semanticCore: Weaving closely, plaiting with gold or gems, interlocking rings of mail, or nested structures

keyDerivations.meaning: Closely woven, plaited with gold and jewels, or interlinked neatly

figurativeOrRareUsages: عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مَوْضُونَةٍ (56:15): On thrones woven/decorated (with gold, silk, and precious gems).
summaryEn: The root refers to tight, intricate weaving or plaiting (Wadn), describing the luxurious, gold-woven couches prepared for the foremost believers in Paradise.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر نسج الشيء نَسْجاً متداخلاً دقيقاً محكماً مرصعاً بالذهب واللؤلؤ (وضن)، واصفاً سرر أهل الجنة (موضونة) لبديع صنعها وجمال نسجها.
rawText: مِيضَنَةٌ i. q. قُفَّةٌ i. e. مَرْجُونَةٌ. (TA in art. ضون.) وَضِينٌ A girth (بِطَان) wide, woven of thongs or hair, or only of skin; (M, K;) said to be adapted for the رَحْل and the هَوْدَج; the بِطَان being peculiarly for the قَتَب: (M:) or it is for the هَوْدَج, like the بِطَان for the قَتَب and the تَصْدِير for the رَحْل and the حِزَام for the سَرْج; and both [but to what this refers is doubtful] are like the نِسْع, except that they are of thongs woven one upon another, doubly or more. (S.)
